Personal reference · boat electronics

Axopar 28 Cabin — Martin's engine setup

Where the Mercury V8 300 plugs in, and what to buy depending on what's already at the helm.

BoatAxopar 28 Cabin
EngineMercury V8 300 hp
Model year2020
Engine serial2B739561
01 — Layout

Where the plug actually is

MERCURY V8 300 · 2020SN 2B739561
10-PIN SMARTCRAFT PORTunder helm dash
Brass = the engine, on the transom Teal = the connector everything below plugs into
02 — Options

What plugs into that port

Start here

VesselView Mobile

~$275 · Bluetooth module

Plugs straight into the SmartCraft harness at the diagnostic port. No boat wiring, DIY install, mounts anywhere below the helm.

  • RPM, fuel flow & economy, engine temp
  • Battery voltage, engine hours
  • Fault codes & maintenance alerts
  • Free iOS / Android app, GPS trip log
Best for: Martin, wanting engine health on his phone, nothing else installed.

SmartCraft Connect

SCC-1 gateway · installer job

Converts SmartCraft data to NMEA 2000, so it shows up on an existing chartplotter instead of a phone.

  • RPM, speed, fuel flow, temp, trim
  • Alarms shown live on the MFD
  • Needs an NMEA 2000 backbone already on board
  • May need adapter + J-box for the network
Best for: if the Axopar already runs a Garmin / Simrad / Raymarine screen at the helm.

Mercury CDS G3

Dealer / workshop tool

What a Mercury-authorized mechanic plugs into the same port during a service visit. Not something an owner buys.

  • Full diagnostics + fault code clearing
  • ECU-level work, warranty service
  • Laptop-based, dealer-only software
Best for: knowing what the shop is doing — not a purchase for Martin.
03 — Decision

Ask Martin this one question

If the Axopar already has a chartplotter at the helm
Get SmartCraft Connect — engine data lands where he already looks.
If there's no dedicated screen, or he wants it on his phone anyway
Get VesselView Mobile — plugs in himself, $275, no wiring.
04 — Going deeper

Can Martin buy his way past VesselView?

Short version: the dealer tool is a dead end for an individual — but there's a real DIY middle tier.

~€249
SmartCraft Connect + NMEA2000 logger

e.g. Yacht Devices YDEG-04. Same data as VesselView (RPM, temps, fuel, alarms) on a chartplotter or PC, logged over time. Not deeper fault codes — SmartCraft is proprietary, generic loggers can't pull ECU trouble codes.

More logging, same depth
~$600–750
Rinda Diacom + laptop + CAN adapter

Real active/stored fault codes, live sensor data, engine hours, base timing, runtime logging. Confirmed coverage for 2018+ V8 Verado/FourStroke — matches Martin's engine. What most DIY owners on forums actually bought.

DIY sweet spot
~$2,800–2,900
CDI Electronics M.E.D.S. (Mercury + G3)

Full dealer-grade reads: ECU data, complete fault/trouble codes, freeze-frame, run-time history. Sold openly on retail marine-parts sites, no dealer account needed — but pricey.

If money's no object
~$1,500 + subscription
Mercury CDS G3 (official)

Officially dealer-accounts-only: license tied to a dealer ID + phone number, re-validates online every ~45 days (5-day grace, then it locks). Right-to-repair pressure is loosening this slowly, but there's no clean consumer checkout today. Grey-market license sellers exist — treat as unreliable.

Not realistically available

Warranty: passive, read-only tools (Diacom, M.E.D.S., NMEA2000 loggers) are very unlikely to void Mercury's warranty — the real risk is writing/reflashing the ECU, or a non-Mercury part causing a failure. Ask a dealer directly if Martin wants certainty.

(Mercury + G3) yachtd.com — YDEG-04 Engine Gateway manual mercurymarine.com — Mercury Limited Warranty

Compiled 2026-07-04 for Jacob & Martin. Port location is typical for SmartCraft-equipped Mercury installs — confirm the exact spot on this boat, and re-check prices with a dealer before buying.
